results.question: Which business received the highest average rating between January 1, 2016 and June 30, 2016, and what category does it belong to? Consider only businesses with at least 5 reviews.

args.sql:
SELECT business_ref, AVG(rating) AS avg_rating, COUNT(*) AS review_count
FROM review
WHERE COALESCE(
    TRY_STRPTIME(date, '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'),
    TRY_STRPTIME(date, '%B %d, %Y at %I:%M %p'),
    TRY_STRPTIME(date, '%d %B %Y, %H:%M'),
    TRY_STRPTIME(date, '%d %b %Y, %H:%M'),
    TRY_STRPTIME(date, '%B %d, %Y at %H:%M'),
    TRY_STRPTIME(date, '%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S')
) BETWEEN '2016-01-01' AND '2016-06-30'
GROUP BY business_ref
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5
ORDER BY avg_rating DESC, business_ref ASC
LIMIT 1

SELECT business_ref, AVG(rating) AS avg_rating, COUNT(*) AS review_count
FROM review
WHERE COALESCE(
    TRY_STRPTIME(date, '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'),
    TRY_STRPTIME(date, '%B %d, %Y at %I:%M %p'),
    TRY_STRPTIME(date, '%d %B %Y, %H:%M'),
    TRY_STRPTIME(date, '%d %b %Y, %H:%M'),
    TRY_STRPTIME(date, '%B %d, %Y at %H:%M'),
    TRY_STRPTIME(date, '%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S')
) BETWEEN '2016-01-01' AND '2016-06-30'
GROUP BY business_ref
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5
ORDER BY avg_rating DESC, business_ref ASC

WITH parsed AS (
  SELECT "Index", 
    COALESCE(
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%B %d, %Y at %I:%M %p'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%d %b %Y, %H:%M'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%d %B %Y, %H:%M'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%B %d, %Y at %H:%M'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S')
    ) AS dt,
    "Open", "High", "Low"
  FROM index_trade
  WHERE COALESCE(
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%B %d, %Y at %I:%M %p'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%d %b %Y, %H:%M'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%d %B %Y, %H:%M'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%B %d, %Y at %H:%M'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S')
    ) >= DATE '2020-01-01'
),
volatility AS (
  SELECT "Index", AVG(("High" - "Low") / NULLIF("Open", 0)) AS avg_intraday_volatility
  FROM parsed
  WHERE "Index" IN ('HSI', 'NSE', 'KRX', 'TWSE')
  GROUP BY "Index"
)
SELECT v."Index", i."Exchange", v.avg_intraday_volatility
FROM volatility v
JOIN index_info i ON 
  (v."Index" = 'HSI' AND i."Exchange" = 'Hong Kong Stock Exchange') OR
  (v."Index" = 'NSE' AND i."Exchange" = 'National Stock Exchange of India') OR
  (v."Index" = 'KRX' AND i."Exchange" = 'Korea Exchange') OR
  (v."Index" = 'TWSE' AND i."Exchange" = 'Taiwan Stock Exchange')
ORDER BY v.avg_intraday_volatility DESC
LIMIT 1;

results.question: Among North American stock indices, which indices had more up days than down days in 2018?

args.sql:
WITH parsed AS (
  SELECT "Index", 
    COALESCE(
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%B %d, %Y at %I:%M %p'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%d %b %Y, %H:%M'),

      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%B %d, %Y at %H:%M'),

    ) AS dt,
    "Open", "Close"
  FROM index_trade
  WHERE "Date" LIKE '2018%'
),
up_down_counts AS (
  SELECT "Index",
    SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days,

  FROM parsed
  WHERE dt IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY "Index"
)
SELECT * FROM up_down_counts

WITH parsed AS (
  SELECT "Index", 
    COALESCE(
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%B %d, %Y at %I:%M %p'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%d %b %Y, %H:%M'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%d %B %Y, %H:%M'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%B %d, %Y at %H:%M'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S')
    ) AS dt,
    "Open", "Close"
  FROM index_trade
  WHERE dt IS NOT NULL
),
up_down_counts AS (
  SELECT "Index",
    SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days,
    SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days
  FROM parsed
  WHERE dt >= '2018-01-01' AND dt < '2019-01-01'
  GROUP BY "Index"
),
na_exchanges AS (
  SELECT 'New York Stock Exchange' AS Exchange UNION ALL
  SELECT 'NASDAQ' UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Toronto Stock Exchange'
),
na_indices AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT "Index"
  FROM index_trade
  WHERE "Index" IN (
    SELECT "Index"
    FROM index_trade
    WHERE "Index" IN (
      SELECT "Index"
      FROM index_trade
      LIMIT 0
    )
  )
),
eligible_indices AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT it."Index"
  FROM index_trade it
  JOIN index_info ii ON
    (ii.Exchange IN ('New York Stock Exchange', 'NASDAQ', 'Toronto Stock Exchange'))
  WHERE it."Index" IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT udc."Index"
FROM up_down_counts udc
JOIN (
  SELECT DISTINCT it."Index"
  FROM index_trade it
  JOIN index_info ii ON
    (ii.Exchange IN ('New York Stock Exchange', 'NASDAQ', 'Toronto Stock Exchange'))
  WHERE it."Index" IS NOT NULL
) eligible ON udc."Index" = eligible."Index"
WHERE udc.up_days > udc.down_days
ORDER BY udc."Index" ASC;

args.sql:
WITH parsed AS (
  SELECT "Index",
    COALESCE(
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%B %d, %Y at %I:%M %p'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%d %b %Y, %H:%M'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%d %B %Y, %H:%M'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%B %d, %Y at %H:%M'),
      TRY_STRPTIME("Date", '%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S')
    ) AS dt,
    "CloseUSD"
  FROM index_trade
  WHERE "Date" IS NOT NULL
),
monthly AS (
  SELECT "Index", DATE_TRUNC('month', dt) AS month,
    FIRST("CloseUSD" ORDER BY dt) AS open_price,
    LAST("CloseUSD" ORDER BY dt)  AS close_price
  FROM parsed
  WHERE dt IS NOT NULL AND "CloseUSD" IS NOT NULL AND "CloseUSD" > 0
    AND dt >= '2000-01-01'
  GROUP BY "Index", DATE_TRUNC('month', dt)
),
monthly_returns AS (
  SELECT "Index", month, (close_price / open_price - 1) AS monthly_return
  FROM monthly
  WHERE open_price > 0
),
total_returns AS (
  SELECT "Index", EXP(SUM(LN(1 + monthly_return))) - 1 AS total_return
  FROM monthly_returns
  GROUP BY "Index"
)
SELECT * FROM total_returns
ORDER BY total_return DESC
LIMIT 5;

results.question: Which decade of publication (e.g., 1980s) has the highest average rating among decades with at least 10 distinct books that have been rated? Return the decade with the highest average rating.

args.sql:
WITH book_decades AS (
  SELECT
    book_id,
    title,
    COALESCE(
      CAST(SUBSTRING(details FROM '\m((?:19|20)\d{2})\M') AS INTEGER),
      CAST(SUBSTRING(subtitle FROM '\m((?:19|20)\d{2})\M') AS INTEGER)
    ) AS pub_year
  FROM books_info
  WHERE details IS NOT NULL OR subtitle IS NOT NULL
),
book_decade_ratings AS (
  SELECT
    (pub_year / 10) * 10 AS decade,
    book_id
  FROM book_decades
  WHERE pub_year IS NOT NULL
),
book_avg_ratings AS (
  SELECT
    r.purchase_id,
    AVG(r.rating) AS avg_rating
  FROM review_database.review r
  GROUP BY r.purchase_id
),
joined_books AS (
  SELECT
    bdr.decade,
    bar.avg_rating
  FROM book_decade_ratings bdr
  JOIN book_avg_ratings bar ON bar.purchase_id = REPLACE(bdr.book_id, 'bookid_', 'purchaseid_')
),
decade_stats AS (
  SELECT
    decade,
    COUNT(DISTINCT REPLACE(bar.purchase_id, 'purchaseid_', '')) AS distinct_books,
    AVG(bar.avg_rating) AS decade_avg_rating
  FROM joined_books jb
  JOIN review_database.review bar ON bar.purchase_id = REPLACE(jb.book_id, 'bookid_', 'purchaseid_')
  GROUP BY decade
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT REPLACE(bar.purchase_id, 'purchaseid_', '')) >= 10
)
SELECT decade
FROM decade_stats
ORDER BY decade_avg_rating DESC
LIMIT 1;

args.sql:
WITH volatile_days AS (
  SELECT table_name AS ticker, COUNT(*) AS volatile_day_count
  FROM information_schema.tables
  WHERE table_schema = 'main'
  AND table_name IN (
    SELECT "Symbol" FROM stockinfo WHERE "Market Category" = 'S'
  )
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM "main"."" || table_name || "" AS t
    WHERE COALESCE(TRY_CAST(t."High" AS DOUBLE), 0) - COALESCE(TRY_CAST(t."Low" AS DOUBLE), 0) > 0.2 * COALESCE(TRY_CAST(t."Low" AS DOUBLE), 1)
    AND COALESCE(TRY_STRPTIME(t."Date", '%Y-%m-%d'), NULL) BETWEEN '2019-01-01' AND '2019-12-31'
  )
  GROUP BY table_name
)
SELECT v.ticker, s."Company Description"
FROM volatile_days v
JOIN (
  SELECT "Symbol", "Company Description" FROM stockinfo WHERE "Market Category" = 'S'
) s ON v.ticker = s."Symbol"
ORDER BY v.volatile_day_count DESC
LIMIT 5;
